title: bromus sitchensis var. polyanthus
intro: Bromus sitchensis var. polyanthus, also known as the Sitka brome or Sitka wildrye, is a subspecies of grass that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture or salve form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
